zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Nicholas St john , lord lydiacd-tregoze, high sheriff of wiltshire於1526年11月出生在英國英格蘭維爾爾萊迪亞德 特雷格茲時，他的父親LORD JOHN ST JOHN21歲，母親Margaret Carew16歲。他於大約1547年與Lady elizabeth blount , lady lydiard- trogoze在英國英格蘭奧可福爾馬普萊度哈姆結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他去世於1589年11月8日在英國維爾爾萊迪亞德 特雷格茲享年63歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭維爾爾萊迪亞德 特雷格茲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Nicholas St john , lord lydiacd-tregoze, high sheriff of wiltshire于1526年11月出生在英国英格兰維爾爾萊迪亞德 特雷格茲时，他的父亲LORD JOHN ST JOHN21岁，母亲Margaret Carew16岁。他于大约1547年与Lady elizabeth blount , lady lydiard- trogoze在英国英格兰奧可福爾馬普萊度哈姆结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和6个女儿的父母。他去世于1589年11月8日在英国維爾爾萊迪亞德 特雷格茲享年63岁，埋葬在英国英格兰維爾爾萊迪亞德 特雷格茲。

nameMeanings.name: St
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of the numerous places in France so called from the dedication of their churches to St. Jean (  see  John  ).
2  Americanized form of French   St  .   Jean  .

variants.name: John
meanings: English and Welsh: ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Yoḥanan ‘Jehovah has favored (me with a son)’ or ‘may Jehovah favor (this child)’. This personal name was adopted into Latin (via Greek) as Johannes, and has enjoyed enormous popularity in Europe throughout the Christian era, being given in honor of Saint John the Baptist, precursor of Christ, and of Saint John the Evangelist, author of the fourth gospel, as well as the nearly one thousand other Christian saints who bore the name. Some of the principal forms of the personal name in other languages are: Welsh Ieuan, Evan, Siôn, and Ioan; Scottish Ia(i)n; Irish Séan; German Johann, Johannes; Dutch and Slavic Jan; French Jean; Italian Giovanni; Spanish Juan; Portuguese João; Greek Iōannēs (vernacular Giannis, Yannis); Russian Ivan. There were a number of different forms of the name in Middle English, including Jan(e), a male name (see Jayne ); Jen (see Jenkin ); Jon(e) (see Jones ); and Han(n) (see Hann ). By the beginning of the 14th century John rivalled William in popularity and has always been a favorite name. Johan became Jo(h)n, and another Old French form Jehan was shortened to Jan and Jen, giving rise to Old French and Middle English diminutives such as Jonin, Janin, and Jenin. More common in Middle English were Jankin, Jonkin, and Jenkin, which were Middle Dutch pet forms introduced after the Conquest by Flemish and Picard settlers. The most common pet form of John was Jack, another borrowing from Flemish and Picard usage. Han may sometimes have been a short form of Johan but was more usually a pet form of Henry. There were also various Middle English feminine versions of this name (e.g. Joan, Jehan), some of which were indistinguishable from masculine forms. The distinction on grounds of gender between John and Joan was not firmly established in English until the 17th century. It was even later that Jean and Jane were specialized as specifically female names in English; bearers of these surnames and their derivatives are more likely to derive them from a male ancestor than a female. As a surname in the British Isles, John is particularly frequent in Wales, where it is a late formation representing Welsh Siôn rather than the older form Ieuan (which gave rise to the surname Evan ). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean Youkhana , French Jean , Hungarian János (see Janos ), Slovenian Janež and Janeš (see Janes ), Czech Jan , Albanian Gjoni , and their derivatives (see examples at Johnson ). The name John is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Ninan and Yohannan ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.
German: from a North German and Silesian variant of the personal name   Johannes  . This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare   Yohn  .

variants.name: Jean
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Francois, Marcel, Yves, Andre, Jacques, Georges, Michel, Philippe, Serge, Yvon, Cecile.

French, Walloon, West Indian (mainly Haiti), Mauritian, and English (of French Huguenot origin): from the male personal name Jean, French form of John . This is one of the most frequent surnames in Haiti (along with Joseph and Pierre ). It is also established elsewhere in the West Indies (e.g. in the Dominican Republic, Saint Lucia, Saint Martin, and Bahamas).
English: habitational name for someone from Genoa in Italy; compare   Janeway  .

nameMeanings.name: john
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Welsh: ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Yoḥanan ‘Jehovah has favored (me with a son)’ or ‘may Jehovah favor (this child)’. This personal name was adopted into Latin (via Greek) as Johannes, and has enjoyed enormous popularity in Europe throughout the Christian era, being given in honor of Saint John the Baptist, precursor of Christ, and of Saint John the Evangelist, author of the fourth gospel, as well as the nearly one thousand other Christian saints who bore the name. Some of the principal forms of the personal name in other languages are: Welsh Ieuan, Evan, Siôn, and Ioan; Scottish Ia(i)n; Irish Séan; German Johann, Johannes; Dutch and Slavic Jan; French Jean; Italian Giovanni; Spanish Juan; Portuguese João; Greek Iōannēs (vernacular Giannis, Yannis); Russian Ivan. There were a number of different forms of the name in Middle English, including Jan(e), a male name (see Jayne ); Jen (see Jenkin ); Jon(e) (see Jones ); and Han(n) (see Hann ). By the beginning of the 14th century John rivalled William in popularity and has always been a favorite name. Johan became Jo(h)n, and another Old French form Jehan was shortened to Jan and Jen, giving rise to Old French and Middle English diminutives such as Jonin, Janin, and Jenin. More common in Middle English were Jankin, Jonkin, and Jenkin, which were Middle Dutch pet forms introduced after the Conquest by Flemish and Picard settlers. The most common pet form of John was Jack, another borrowing from Flemish and Picard usage. Han may sometimes have been a short form of Johan but was more usually a pet form of Henry. There were also various Middle English feminine versions of this name (e.g. Joan, Jehan), some of which were indistinguishable from masculine forms. The distinction on grounds of gender between John and Joan was not firmly established in English until the 17th century. It was even later that Jean and Jane were specialized as specifically female names in English; bearers of these surnames and their derivatives are more likely to derive them from a male ancestor than a female. As a surname in the British Isles, John is particularly frequent in Wales, where it is a late formation representing Welsh Siôn rather than the older form Ieuan (which gave rise to the surname Evan ). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean Youkhana , French Jean , Hungarian János (see Janos ), Slovenian Janež and Janeš (see Janes ), Czech Jan , Albanian Gjoni , and their derivatives (see examples at Johnson ). The name John is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Ninan and Yohannan ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.
German: from a North German and Silesian variant of the personal name   Johannes  . This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare   Yohn  .

variants.name: Ancar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Francois, Alphonse, Etienne, Fabien, Mireille.

Probably an altered form of French (Pas-de-Calais, Nord) Ancart, itself a variant of Hancart and Hanecart: from a pet form (double diminutive) of the personal name Han, a short form of French Jehan and Flemish Johan(nes) (see John ).

variants.name: Bongiovanni
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Mario, Antonio, Dante, Salvatore, Santo, Antonino, Domenic, Gaetano, Geno, Giacomo, Gilda, Luciano, Nino.
Italian: from a personal name composed of the elements bon ‘good’ + Giovanni, an equivalent of John .

variants.name: Di Giovanna
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Gaspar, Alfonso, Carlo, Domenic, Gaetano, Liborio, Nunzio, Pietro, Rosario, Sal, Salvatore.
Italian: metronymic from the female personal name Giovanna, an equivalent of Joan (see John ).

variants.name: Anes
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Angel, Dionisio, Esperanza, Gilberto, Ines, Juanita, Luis, Luisa, Manuel, Manuela, Pedro.  Portuguese Joao.
Portuguese and Galician: patronymic from a shortened form of the Latin personal name Johannes (Portuguese João, Galician Xoán; see John ).

variants.name: Burhans
meanings: German: from Middle Low German būr ‘farmer, peasant’ + the personal name Hans, a short form of Johannes (see John ), hence a distinguishing occupational name ‘farmer John’ with Hans being a very frequent name. See also Burian .

variants.name: Breihan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Kurt.
German: distinguishing nickname for a (beer) brewer, from Middle High German briuw(er) ‘brew(er)’ + a short form of the personal name Johann(es) (see John ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: English (Gloucestershire) and Welsh (Monmouthshire): from the Middle English male personal name Jan(e), from Old French Jehan, a variant of Johan, which gave rise to the common alternative John . Sometimes the name may also arise from the Welsh variant Ieun of Welsh Ieuan, likewise equivalent to John (compare Evan ).

variants.name: Di Giovanni
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Salvatore, Vito, Rocco, Sal, Aldo, Carlo, Gino, Giuseppe, Guido, Agostino, Carmela.
Italian: patronymic from the personal name Giovanni, an equivalent of John . Compare De Giovanni , Dejohn , and Giovanni .

variants.name: Eanes
meanings: English (Hampshire): probably a variant of Heanes with loss of initial H-, itself a variant of Heane, with post-medieval excrescent -s, a nickname from Middle English hen(e), heine ‘poor, needy, wretched’ (Old English hēan). Eanes is now rare in Britain, and in the US is found predominantly in VA.
Portuguese and Galician: patronymic from the Latin personal name Johannes (Portuguese João, Galician Xoán; see John ).

variants.name: Ene
meanings: Romanian: from a derivative of the personal name Ioan (see John ).
West African (Nigeria): unexplained.

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nickolas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: variant spelling of   Nicholas  , influenced by the short form   Nick .

variants.name: Nicolas
meanings: From Late Greek. See Nicholas .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Nicholas St john , lord lydiacd-tregoze, high sheriff of wiltshire was born in November 1526, in Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, LORD JOHN ST JOHN, was 21 and his mother, Margaret Carew, was 16. He married Lady elizabeth blount , lady lydiard- trogoze about 1547, in Mapledurham, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 6 daughters. He died on 8 November 1589, in Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, England, at the age of 63, and was buried in Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Nicholas St john , lord lydiacd-tregoze, high sheriff of wiltshire est né en novembre 1526, à Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, LORD JOHN ST JOHN, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Margaret Carew, avait 16 ans. Il a épousé Lady elizabeth blount , lady lydiard- trogoze environ 1547, à Mapledurham, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 6 filles. Il est décédé le 8 novembre 1589, à Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterré à Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Nicholas St john , lord lydiacd-tregoze, high sheriff of wiltshire im November 1526 in Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater LORD JOHN ST JOHN 21 und seine Mutter Margaret Carew 16. Er hat um 1547 in Mapledurham, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom Lady elizabeth blount , lady lydiard- trogoze geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Er starb am 8. November 1589 im Alter von 63 Jahren in Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, England und wurde in Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Nicholas St john , lord lydiacd-tregoze, high sheriff of wiltshire è nato il nel novembre 1526 nel Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, LORD JOHN ST JOHN, aveva 21 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Carew, aveva 16 anni. Lui sposato Lady elizabeth blount , lady lydiard- trogoze circa 1547, nel Mapledurham, Oxfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 6 figlie. Morì il 8 novembre 1589, nel Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, England, all'età di 63 anni, e fu sepolto a Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Nicholas St john , lord lydiacd-tregoze, high sheriff of wiltshireが1526年11月にイギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアリディアルド トレゴズで生まれた時，父LORD JOHN ST JOHNは21歳，母Margaret Carewは16歳であった。約1547年にイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアマプレドウルハムでLady elizabeth blount , lady lydiard- trogozeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼は1589年11月8日にイギリスウィルトシュアイアリディアルド トレゴズで63歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアリディアルド トレゴズにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Nicholas St john , lord lydiacd-tregoze, high sheriff of wiltshire(이)가 1526년 11월 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 리디아르드 트레고즈에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 LORD JOHN ST JOHN(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Carew(은)는 16세였다. 그는 대략 1547년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 마플레더람에서 Lady elizabeth blount , lady lydiard- trogoze(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1589년 11월 8일 영국 윌츠히르 리디아르드 트레고즈에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 리디아르드 트레고즈에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Nicholas St john , lord lydiacd-tregoze, high sheriff of wiltshire nasceu em novembro de 1526, em Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, LORD JOHN ST JOHN, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Carew, tinha 16 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady elizabeth blount , lady lydiard- trogoze aproximadamente 1547, em Mapledurham, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 6 filhas. Ele faleceu em 8 de novembro de 1589, em Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, com 63 anos, e foi sepultado em Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Nicholas St john , lord lydiacd-tregoze, high sheriff of wiltshire родился ноябрь 1526 г. в местности лидярд трегоз, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени LORD JOHN ST JOHN было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Margaret Carew, 16. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady elizabeth blount , lady lydiard- trogoze около 1547 г. в местности мапледурам, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 6 дочерей. Он умер 8 ноября 1589 г. в местности лидярд трегоз, уилцхир, Англия в возрасте 63 лет и был похоронен в местности лидярд трегоз, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Nicholas St john , lord lydiacd-tregoze, high sheriff of wiltshire nació en noviembre de 1526, en Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, LORD JOHN ST JOHN, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Margaret Carew, tenía 16 años. Se casó con Lady elizabeth blount , lady lydiard- trogoze aproximadamente 1547, en Mapledurham, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió el 8 de noviembre de 1589, en Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 63 años, y fue sepultado en Lydiard Tregoze, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
